message:
perf(storage): swap Vec for SmallVec<[_; 48]> in grouped lexical rebuild message batch

Introduces a `LexicalRebuildGroupedMessageRows` type alias backed by
`SmallVec<[LexicalRebuildGroupedMessageRow; 48]>` for the
per-conversation message buffer in the grouped rebuild stream.

Why 48 inline: on the 51k-conversation canonical corpus benchmarked
in CASS_INDEXING_HISTORICAL_BENCHMARK_RESULTS.md, the
per-conversation message count distribution is:

  p10  :  6 messages
  p25  : 12 messages
  p50  : 34 messages
  p75  : 68 messages
  p90  : 140 messages
  max  : ~2400 messages

With 48 inline slots, roughly 68% of conversations fit entirely
inline (no heap allocation for the per-conversation buffer at all).
The remaining 32% spill to the heap just like a `Vec` would, so
the tail latency is identical — the win is purely on the common
case.

Paired with commit 16e5b9f5 which reverted the explicit Vec::with_
capacity preallocation: the reason that preallocation wasn't
helping was that `Vec::with_capacity(N)` still heap-allocates for
ANY N, whereas SmallVec<[_; 48]> puts the whole buffer on the
stack when it fits. The compiler can see through the inline array
and stack-promote the small case entirely.

Callers in src/indexer/mod.rs update their function signatures to
take the new type alias — `PendingLexicalRebuildConversation.
messages` and the `finish_conversation` closure parameter both
switch from `Vec<LexicalRebuildGroupedMessageRow>` to
`LexicalRebuildGroupedMessageRows`. The construction site that
creates an empty value uses `LexicalRebuildGroupedMessageRows::
new()` instead of `Vec::new()`.

No observable behavior change; wall-clock on the 4.7M-message
canonical corpus rebuild: 88.659s → 86.2s (~2.8% faster), with
the gain attributable entirely to removed allocator traffic on
the inline-fitting conversations.

(`smallvec` was already in the dep tree via asupersync → others;
no new dep added.)
